Transcrição Completa: How to Make a lot of Money w Webinars w Ben Bader
[00:00] "Thank you for the intro. Um, so here's how we made a lot of money on webinars. Um, I asked Jeremy if I could go first just because in case this sucks, then expectations are low and it can only get better."
[00:15] "Um, so for context, um, we've been in the info space running live webinars with our clients weekly or bi-weekly for about a year and a half. Um, and most of our clients are are making anywhere from 200 to 400K a month."
[00:30] "Our biggest client has gotten up to a little over 600k a month. Um, and our biggest webinar ever was for this crypto offer actually where we did like 170 or 180 uh collected in about an hour and a half."
[00:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Imagens de Russell Brunson e Jason Fladlin
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Duas fotos lado a lado de Russell Brunson e Jason Fladlin, apresentados como referências principais
[00:50] "Um, so before I get into it, I want to say these two people right here that oddly look like really similar. Um, everything that I'm going to go over has just been regurgitated from them and then somebody else learns it and then it gets to me and then it gets to my clients."
[01:05] "Um, obviously you know this guy Russell Brunson and this is Jason Fladdlin. Um, he's got a book called One to Many and he's made like $100 million or something like that on webinars. So, another good person to to learn from."
[01:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Ugly Slides, Pretty Profits"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Slide simples, sem formatação profissional, demonstrando o conceito de relatabilidade
[01:25] "Um, and like almost everything that I'm talking about, it's it's just the principles that these two and other people that have iterated on on what they've done um have followed. So, as you can see, this isn't the most pretty presentation ever."
[01:40] "Um, so we have a a saying. I think relatability is a really big thing in the info space in general. Um, so if we've had guys that'll show up to a to a webinar and they've got a really professional camera, they've got really good slides, and it almost puts them on a pedestal compared to their audience where they're no longer relatable."
[02:00] "So, for a lot of our clients, like when they try to overdo it, we tell them to kind of tone it down and tell them like, 'Okay, the the uglier the slides, the more you look like this kid or this this dude that just stumbled into what you're doing and what you're about to to present, the better.'"
[02:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What I'm about to show you made our clients over $7 million in 2023"
NÚMEROS: $7 million+
VISUAL: Declaração de resultado financeiro em destaque
[02:22] "Um, so ugly slides, pretty profits, as as you can see in uh in my presentation. Um, so what I'm about to show you made our clients over $7 million in 2023. We lost track at 7 million. It's probably a bit more."

[03:00] "So, like here I'm pre-framing. Obviously, I don't have anything to pitch you at the end, otherwise that'd be that'd be fun. Um, but I'm kind of coming in pre-framing, capturing your attention like, okay, here's why you should listen to me. Here's why I'm credible."

[03:48] "You you want to get on NMI or you want you take payments through crypto. So, our biggest screenshots, our biggest webinar days, I don't have uh, Stripe screenshots because they got kicked off of Stripe and they're using NMI."

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

[04:22] "You want to get them to stop scrolling on their phone. Like odds are it's a 7 p.m. on a random Wednesday and some kid, some 19-year-old kid in Utah is like tuning in um and and seeing how he can start making money online and like he's pro probably got six other tabs open."
[04:40] "So, first thing we try to do is like set the frame, set the frame, get their attention, and get them bought in, get them commenting in the chat. So, we'll all of our uh webinars are through webinar jam."
[04:52] "Um, and we'll say something like this. Comment yes if if you're ready to learn. This is also kind of getting them in that that state where they're uh you're preframing the yes."
[05:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Let's make a deal. Pretend you just paid $2,500 to get this information"
NÚMEROS: $2,500
VISUAL: Frame de valor e compromisso psicológico
[05:06] "So, throughout the presentation, we have oh, comment one if this is making sense. comment. Yes, if you could see yourself doing that. So, it's almost like I call it manipulation. I I think it it is a a level of manipulation, but that's sales. That's that's how a lot of this game works."
[05:25] "Um, so another slide that we have is something like this. So, let's make a deal. Pretend you just paid $2,500 to get this information because other others have."
[05:36] "And then we'll have our client tell a little story about how when they first started off, they um they had somebody that would come to them and and usually this is this is true. Um they'd have somebody that would come to them and and ask for help and then they'd charge them 2500 or three grand for like an hourong call for the information that I'm about to give to you tonight for free."
[05:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "If you don't pay, you don't pay attention"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Quote motivacional sobre compromisso
[06:02] "That's how we frame that. So we also have this quote, if you don't pay, you don't pay attention. So, we have them like close their eyes and and imagine they just spent all of this money to be here again because then they're going to actually pay attention."
[06:18] "Then they feel like they're bought in. And um a lot of it, I mean, if you if you tell somebody, let's say like this watch, it's a $50,000 day date. If I told you that and you didn't know what this was beforehand, you'd see me wearing this watch and you'd be like, 'Okay, like that's that's a sick watch.'"
[06:38] "Like, but if I told you it's a $50 random little uh gas station watch, just your perception of it and your perception of me going into it is going to be very different."
[06:48] "So, if somebody feels like they're about to get this life-changing information, and obviously to be fair, the the training, the content has to be good. Um, but going into into the actual training, you have to make them feel like they are getting access to something that's going to be revolutionary and that's what's actually going to get them to to listen."
[07:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Should you run webinars?"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Título de seção sobre qualificação para webinars
[07:12] "So, if you are considering running webinars for yourself or for your clients, um, honestly, there's a lot of variables about first of all what you should sell. Um, and then whether it's for you or if it's not for you."
[07:28] "Uh most of the clients that come to us that want to run webinars, we turn them away. Um largely because we have kind of built a model where we're working with these guys that have a big existing personal brand."
[07:42] "Um and we're just helping them build a more efficient conversion mechanism for their existing audience. We're not really running paid. Um we have and that's like that generally comes after we've completely validated the funnel."
[07:56] "We've validated the offer. the whole mechanism itself is is completely rock solid and then we're ready to start putting money into it uh money into ads and uh we also want to get our clients a quick win before we have them start spending on ads."
[08:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What should you sell?"
NÚMEROS: $500 to $2,000 (mid-ticket), $3K to $10K (high-ticket)
VISUAL: Comparação entre ofertas mid-ticket e high-ticket
[08:15] "So first what should you sell if you're considering running a a live webinar? So we've done two models. At first it was all the like mid-t offers where it's a course uh $500 to $2,000 and that's at the end of the presentation obviously."
[08:32] "Um the sales page is super simple. You just like drop the link in the chat at the end of the of the webinar and then people can go out and check out and buy."
[08:42] "Um and then we also recently started doing the model where we push like a a high ticket or a call at the end. Um and that's generally just like with the calendar link with a a couple qualifiers and that's that's been really successful for a couple of our newer clients as well."
[08:58] "Um, so and those will be for offers anywhere from like three to 10K. We did have one guy who sold a $3,000 offer at um at the end and it was just like a simple checkout form as well, but he was in like a unique situation where his audience was already very warm and he was just attracting a higher like a a better quality demographic of people."
[09:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Why should you run webinars?"
NÚMEROS: 10 minutes (short form) vs 60-90 minutes (webinar)
VISUAL: Comparação de tempo de exposição e trust building
[09:25] "Um, so that's why we were able to get away with that. But for a lot of the time, like most of the offers we're working with are going to be like BTOC, like here's how to make money, here's how to make your first 10K a month online, like those types of people. It's good to have something in the $500 to $2,000 range."
[09:42] "Um, and it's also a really good mechanism to just get people bought in and trusting you uh before if eventually you do have a high ticket offer that they can buy after. Um, it's a good way to to just build that top of funnel, get people in and get them uh making some money."
[09:58] "So, why should you run webinars? Uh, the trust hour rule is something that, so my friend Mason, who is also a client of mine, he's in the inner circle, uh, but he's back in Canada right now."
[10:12] "Um, he kind of came up with this idea where a lot of his, uh, a lot of his leads, a lot of his audience came from short form content. So, think about somebody if they've watched even 10 short form videos, call it they they've spent maybe 10 minutes consuming your content."
[10:30] "Um, obviously you can get into YouTube, you can get into long form content, but the majority of of his $600,000 a month coaching business was built off of minutelong Tik Toks that he would make in his kitchen."
[10:44] "Um, so the reason the webinar has been super effective for him is because he'll get people in and they consume 10 minutes of content and then you have an hour long, hour and a half long training where now it's like a speed date."
[10:58] "You sat down, you had drinks, you you had a good dinner and now you're in a very different position. You guys actually know each other. Um, and in this case, maybe you're ready to maybe they're ready to buy from you. So, it's just like a speed date."
[11:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Simplifies the sales process"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Conceito de leads mais qualificados para closers
[11:16] "Um, and then it simplifies the sales process. Everybody likes to talk about shitty closers and shitty setters. And this is a way to either cut them out or give them leads that are like absolute layowns because if you do it where um at the end you're directing them to book a call, they've still sat through an hour, hour and a half long training and they know what they're getting themselves into."
[11:38] "Um, so they're going to show up to that call very different from somebody who's like watched 10 minutes worth of videos."
[11:45] "Um, and then lastly, it's efficient if you have a big net of people. Obviously, you can sell to a lot of people at one time. So rather than taking 200 sales calls, you can get 2,000 people on on a webinar and um convert more efficiently than you would if you had to take sales calls individually."
[12:05] "Um, and then also it's a lower commitment than getting on a sales call. So like think about the process for somebody to get on a like to to go through the funnel, opt in, watch the VSSL, book a call, find a find a time on their calendar, and then book a call."
[12:22] "Like there's some that that's that's a big commitment. They have to make sure that they're not taking their kid to soccer practice or they they have to actually put time on their calendar. They have to show up, talk to somebody. they know it's a sales call and that's like kind of nerve-wracking."
[12:38] "So you everybody that has a big audience naturally has people that have like thought about doing that exact thing but they haven't pulled the trigger because they don't want to commit to that."
[12:48] "Um which is why the webinars can be a perfect model because it's a lower commitment. It's like okay I'll fuck sign up for this thing. Maybe I'll show up. Maybe I won't."
[12:58] "um and then they get absolutely harassed by our email and SMS and they end up they end up showing up and uh then now you have that opportunity to sell them."
[13:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "People that shouldn't run webinars"
NÚMEROS: 16 people (exemplo de webinar pequeno)
VISUAL: Contraindicações para o modelo de webinar
[13:10] "So there's also people that shouldn't run webinars um if whenever we have prospects come to us and again because we're mainly reliant on on an existing organic audience. Um if they just don't have enough eyeballs, it's not going to work."
[13:25] "Um it's like you throw a big party and nobody shows up. um kind of embarrassing. You don't want you don't want to run a webinar with 16 people in the live room."
[13:35] "Um you want I I'll get into the exact numbers in a bit, but you just need people that that are going to sign up and going to show up."
[13:42] "And then also, this is rare. Like you can get over a boring personality, but I've had like one or two clients where all the numbers have made sense. Like we got a good amount of registrance, we had a good offer, everything was everything was solid and dialed and it was just a fuck snoozefest."
[14:00] "Like if they were selling like a sleep offer, maybe it would have worked. But if if you do need a bit of a personality, you need to be likable. Obviously, people buy from people they they know, like, and trust."
[14:12] "And when you do have that good personality, you do have that good client or good brand that that can be in front of the camera, that just 10xes everything."
[14:22] "Um because again like you're you're building so much credibility and and you become one with your like they your audience then knows your entire journey and if it's going to be a super boring webinar you can kind of get around it."
[14:35] "Um but if if your client's old and and doesn't have much charisma it probably isn't the best fit."
[14:42] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "KPIs we aim for on webinars"
NÚMEROS:
Registrants: 300-500 minimum
Show up rate: 30-50%
Conversion rate: 5-10% (direct sale), 10%+ (call booking)
VISUAL: Métricas-chave de performance para webinars
[14:45] "Um so KPIs we aim for on the webinars. So registrants as many as possible like this is it's simply a numbers game for us at this point because everything else is going to stay consistent."
[14:58] "We know how most of them are going to convert. We know we know how people are going to show up and and convert and so on. So we want to get a minimum of at least 3 to 500 registrants uh just to make it worthwhile."
[15:12] "So generally we charge 15k and 15% when we build out a new a new webinar for a funnel or for a client. Um, so for them to like at least break even and hopefully see a a two to 5x ROI on their first webinar, we need at least 300 to 500 uh registrants. Again, hopefully more."
[15:32] "Uh, show up rate, you're going to get at least 30%. If you are harassing them properly, harassing the the opt-ins properly. Um, but with organic traffic, it can be as high as as 40 to 50%."
[15:45] "Um, again, because those people are warm, like this is this is an opportunity they've been waiting for. It's like, all right, I'm thinking about booking a call, but now this is a lower commitment thing that I can do."
[15:58] "Uh, stay through rate is definitely something that other people track and we probably should be tracking. So, that means like how many people stay to the offer. Um, but we haven't really tracked that, but I'm sure there's like a good metric for for how many people you should get to stay."
[16:15] "So, uh, I'm sure Jason Flatland or somebody else that I showed you in the beginning has talked about that metric. U, but then conversion rate at the end. So, 5% minimum. We've had as high as as 10%. And that's if you're selling something directly at the end of the webinar."
[16:32] "Um, and then for the webinars that we do a calendar link to book a call, we'll get at least 10% to book a call."
[16:40] "um one of our clients. So, he has like this this Cody Sanchez style offer where it's like buying small local businesses. Um and he was the first one that we really tested out. Let's let's see what happens if if we just direct people to book a call at the end."
[16:55] "I think on our first webinar we ran with him, um we probably had around four to 500 people on and we booked like 90 or probably even 100 calls."
[17:05] "Um, and from that I know he collected six figures from that very first webinar and that blew my mind. Like that's based on that attendee number like that was that was a very very solid webinar."
[17:18] "Uh, and we had 25% of that one. So that uh made us happy as well."
[17:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quick Math"
NÚMEROS:
1,000 registrants
300 show up (30%)
15 purchasers (5%)
$1,000 offer = $15K collected
VISUAL: Cálculo simples de ROI de webinar
[17:25] "So quick math if your offer is a,000 bucks you get a,000 registrants. Call it 300 show up. You'll have 1500 purchasers. Pretty simple. And you'll collect 15K."
[17:38] "Um, that's just kind of how we break it down to our clients. And then on our side, what we're doing is building out the funnel, the entire webinar script, all of the slides, um, and I'll show you our our entire framework for that."
[17:52] "And then all the email and SMS nurture. Obviously, I have spam in there. Um, because again, it it takes a lot to get these people to get people to show up."
[18:03] "Um, and then post webinar emails for people that either attend and don't buy, that attend, and you want to to upsell them on something else or that miss the the webinar completely."
[18:15] "Um, like those three different buckets, those three different avatars are going to get different messaging after the the webinar."

NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Fluxograma do funil: Traffic → Opt-in → Confirmation → Nurture → Live Webinar → Post-webinar
[20:20] "So, building the boat. This is basically how it works. Um, I could probably have like a really cool kind of graphic for this, but uh, basically, like I said, you get a ton of traffic, get them on an opt-in page, uh, on the confirmation page."
[20:35] "So, that's also really important, like pre-framing them, getting them to save the the webinar link, and then getting them into like a Telegram group or an IG broadcast channel. Those have been huge, actually."
[20:48] "Um, that's like a newer thing that Instagram came out with. Um it's like a telegram group on Instagram, especially if they if they have a big audience that that has come through Instagram."
[21:00] "Um so on the confirmation page, we'll try and get them to join that and then they get the link um and then they get the email and SMS nurture, get them on the live webinar and then after the webinar, uh all the email and SMS that continues to go out based on what they do."

[22:25] "Um, so not everybody's perfect, but that's basically how that how that works once they sign up."
[22:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Email Nurture Sequence"
NÚMEROS: 48h, 24h, 12h, 3h reminders
VISUAL: Sequência de emails: Confirmation → Story → Social Proof → YouTube → Reminders (48h, 24h, 12h, 3h) → Live Link
[22:35] "And then what the email nurture looks like. Um, so without going into like all the details of every single email, the goal of email once they sign up is to sell them on coming to the webinar."
[22:48] "So who what did they just sign up for? Why should they be excited? Why should they trust the person that's going to be on the live webinar?"
[22:58] "Um, so they're going to get a confirmation and like little short kind of story email immediately. Sometimes that can be like a link to a YouTube video because like again, we just want them to spend as much time with us as possible."
[23:12] "Like the the trust hour thing like I said earlier, the more time they spend with you, the more likely they're going to be to buy."
[23:20] "So, we'll uh we'll just start warming them up to the to the person that that's going to be presenting on the webinar. We'll send them social proof, uh, YouTube videos, and then obviously this is also contingent on when they sign up because if they sign up a day before, they're not going to get all of those other emails."
[23:38] "Um, but if they sign up a few days before, they're going to get 48 hour reminder, 24-hour reminder, 12 hour, 3 hour, and then the live room link."
[23:48] "Um, so it really is just a numbers game. especially the more the the lower aware the audience is and like the less mature the audience is, I think the more nurture you need."
[24:00] "Um like the guy that I was talking about earlier who has uh like the small business acquisition offer. So he's in his mid30s. His audience is more mature. I'm not spamming them as much as I am these 19-year-old kids that want to learn copyrightiting."
[24:15] "uh just because that would turn them off and they're like more responsible adults that'll actually show up without without getting 37 emails."
[24:25] "Um so that is like kind of play it by ear if you are running it for clients like you don't want the exact same messaging for a mass market B TOC offer that you do for an offer where somebody needs to have at least like 20K liquid to invest."
[24:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "SMS Nurture"
NÚMEROS: 12:00 PM (game day), 1 hour, 15 minutes, 10 minutes reminders
VISUAL: Sequência de SMS: Confirmation → Day-of 12PM → 1 hour → 15 min → Live Now → 10 min Hail Mary
[24:42] "Um, so on the SMS side, again, same same rule applies here, um, depending on on the level of awareness of the the audience, they're going to need either more or less nurture. But generally what that looks like, they get a confirmation message and then, uh, nothing until the day of the webinar."
[25:00] "Uh, just because SMS gets really expensive at scale. Um, and then on the day of the webinar, they're going to get like a 12:00 p.m. kind of hype up text. It's something usually I'll say like first name it's game day."
[25:15] "Uh I got I got your spot reserved tonight 7:00 p.m. be there. Be square. Come with a a smile on your face. Something like that. They're really light, really casual."
[25:25] "This is a just a sample of like the 1 hour reminder that we usually send out. We got a 15-minute reminder live now. And then um if it's kind of slow, like let's say we were expecting 500 attendees and we only have 400 on 10 minutes in, I'll send like a like a Hail Mary at the last second."
[25:45] "Um like one last SMS as well 10 to 15 minutes in to the webinar. And again, all of those are short. They're simple. It's like, hey, you signed up for this thing. Show up link."
[25:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "3 Things You Need to Sell"
NÚMEROS: 3 elementos principais
VISUAL: Lista de três vendas essenciais: 1) Mechanism/Business Model, 2) You/Authority, 3) Them/Belief System
[26:00] "Um and then so the training once you're actually on the live webinar, how does it work? So I think this applies to like any info product no matter how you're selling it. You need to sell them on three things."
[26:12] "You need to sell them on first the mechanism uh the business model. So for example like if you're selling ecom coaching or Amazon FBA or something like that they need to know that that mechanism actually works."
[26:25] "They need to be able to sit down at Thanksgiving dinner and like explain it to their family. They just need to have a basic understanding of it and they need to know that it's not going anywhere."
[26:35] "Like Uncle Sam isn't going to wipe it out. it's not going to like inflation isn't going to isn't going to ruin it. So, it's an opportunity that actually works. Um, and they understand how they can make money with it."
[26:48] "We also had like a a dating offer. So, it was the same kind of thing there. Uh, his he was selling like a cold approach course. Um, so we needed to sell them on why cold approach is the best mechanism versus Hinge or Instagram or like other ways to meet women."
[27:05] "Um so first once they're sold on that mechanism and that's like done mainly through education and I'll I'll talk more about that in a sec. Uh they also need to be sold on you or your client as the personality. So or as the authority rather."
[27:20] "Um obviously again people buy from people they know, like trust. And how are you going to get them to be bought into you? How are you going to be relatable? How are you going to tell stories that show them that you're just this normal person that didn't have any special unique talents or anything that they didn't have?"
[27:38] "Um, and again, the webinar gives you a really it gives you a good chance to be able to to do this."
[27:45] "And then the last thing is the audience's belief system, which is really just selling them on themselves. Um, and the way that we do this is through social proof."
[27:55] "um who are other people that you can show them that have overcome the same limiting beliefs that they had and have went through the same process that they had and uh ended up finding success."
[28:08] "So if you can make these three sales like everything we do on a webinar is is made and and delivered with these three things in mind."
[28:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How the training usually goes"
NÚMEROS: 7:00 PM Eastern (horário padrão), Weekly or Bi-weekly, Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Sunday
VISUAL: Estrutura do webinar ao vivo: Welcome → Pre-frame → Quick Story → Social Proof → Training → Summary → Longer Story → More Social Proof → Gift/Offer
[28:20] "So this is how the training usually goes. Um, so generally just for context, our clients are running them weekly or bi-weekly either Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday or Sunday. Um, almost always at 7:00 p.m. Eastern time and people hop on."
[28:38] "So they get all the they get all the nurture, they hop on the webinar, and client just starts welcoming everybody in. They're probably playing music, vibes are good, got a smile on their face, drinking a bottle of wine."
[28:52] "Um, and then like they just kind of start making small talk with uh with people in the chat, asking them where they're from, uh, what their goals are for 2024, just like keeping it light, getting people excited, and then you start pre-framing."
[29:08] "So, kind of like what what I did a bit earlier, like this this is going to be valuable. Why should you stay to the end? Some of them like do a little giveaway at the end as well."
[29:18] "Um, and then they'll they'll get people to engage in the chat. they'll start talking about uh just like that thing that I showed you earlier with um people have paid me $2,500 for this information that you're going to get tonight for free."
[29:32] "So, just getting them to be bought in and engaged and ready to sit through an hourong presentation."
[29:40] "Um and then we go into the the quick story about our client, about the the presenter. So, he'll basically tell them kind of just like a an intro to him. I used to be here, now I'm here."
[29:52] "Um, and what I'm about to show you tonight is going to show you exactly how I went from there to there to here. Uh, and then a little bit of social proof. Again, you're just selling them on staying till staying through the training."
[30:08] "And then you get into the training and generally what we do here. So, we'll if it's an Amazon FBA webinar, um, we're running one right now and I think it's a five-step training on how to go from beginner to making 10K a month on Amazon or something like that."
[30:25] "And it's like, okay, step one is getting set up on Amazon right way, getting your Amazon seller account set up. Um, step two is getting your LLC or something and then sourcing products, ungating, and then all these other steps."
[30:40] "But what we do is we make it very easy and very digestible in those steps. So like we give them enough to where they feel like they know exactly how they can make money with that thing."
[30:52] "Again, we're trying to sell them on the business model, but there's little nuances and little things that they're not going to know just by watching this hourlong presentation."
[31:02] "Um, after the training, we summarize what they just learned. Again, we like reiterate how valuable it was. And then, uh, you go into the longer story."
[31:12] "So, like, okay, I was I was once in your shoes. I was once where you are right now. I did this. I took these actions and now I have a Lambo and live in Dubai and have 12 girlfriends. Something like that. That's generally how these stories go."
[31:28] "So then you go back into social proof because again selling them on you, selling them on themselves, selling them on the business model, you go back into social proof. This person did this, this person did this, this person did this."
[31:40] "And then you have this gift for them, which is generally the offer. You position it as like, so because you stayed with me for an hourong, hour and a half presentation, I have a gift for you for attending this call tonight. If you want it, comment yes. Comment me in the chat."
[31:58] "So now again, it's like you're you're pre-framing them for the yes. They're they're there's this movie Focus with like Will Smith and Marggo Robbie. Is it Focus? Something like that."
[32:12] "Um where like they they're they're like betting on football or something and uh they get this man to to he sees like the number 55 throughout the day and he's betting this man uh like a million dollars. long story as to how they got there, but he uh got him to say the number 55 and he he like won all of his money back and and it was just because they like programmed him throughout the day to see that number 55."
[32:38] "So, it's the same kind of thing here. If you keep getting people to say yes, hopefully they're going to say yes when you ask them to pull their credit card out and and buy something."
[32:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Value Stack Example"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Estrutura de apresentação da oferta: Gift → Value Stack → Offer Drop → Bonuses → Guarantee
[32:50] "So, after that, after we kind of drop the off or after we uh position it, we go into the value stack. Um, and I'll show you an example of what that actually looks like in a second, but okay."
[33:02] "So, you're going to get this, this, this, you're going to get this, and it's going to show you how to do this. Um, and then we drop the offer, and then actually after the offer drops, we have a few bonuses because, all right, if they if they equate the value, let's say it's $500."
[33:18] "If we just told them exactly what they're going to get, and now they in their head, this thing is worth $500, but because they're on tonight, there's a few special bonuses. there's more value then the value you kind of get them to believe that the value is exceeding the price."
[33:35] "Um so after they after we drop the offer we we show a few special bonuses for attending tonight and then uh they go on to the live Q&A and I'll talk more about that later as well."
[33:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Training, The Story, The Social Proof"
NÚMEROS: 3 elementos de venda
VISUAL: Breakdown dos três componentes: Training (business model), Story (authority/you), Social Proof (them/belief)
[33:50] "Um so the training I I kind of went over these next three slides here but it's where you sell them on the on the business model. You break down the steps into an easy digestible uh system. You give them enough to where they understand it, but you don't give them the whole pie."
[34:08] "The story, that's where you sell them on you. Why you're a good person to learn from, why you're credible, why you're relatable, why it worked for you."
[34:18] "And lastly, the social proof sells them on them. Um, you need to show them that that other people were once in their shoes, same fears, hesitations, and skepticism, and they overcame those beliefs and then ended up finding success."
[34:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Special Offer Slide - Comment ME if you want it"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Slide de engajamento para a oferta, call-to-action para comentar
[34:38] "So, this is generally what the as we're as we're pre-framing the offer, this is usually what it looks like. This is the slide that I what I was telling you about how like, okay, since you sat through this entire presentation, we have a special offer for you. We have a gift for you. Comment me if you want it. Getting them to buy in, be engaged in the chat."
[34:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What You're Getting - Wholesale Real Estate Offer"
NÚMEROS: $497
VISUAL: Breakdown da oferta: módulos, treinamentos, como encontrar motivated sellers, apenas $497
[35:00] "Um, and then this is how we break down the offers usually. So, you're going to get this, this, and this. It's going to show you how to find motivated sellers for this is a a wholesale real estate offer."
[35:12] "Um, basically everything you need to know here, and it's only just $497. So now they have this image of what I just saw was worth $500."
[35:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Special Bonuses - 20 Spots Open"
NÚMEROS: 20 spots
VISUAL: Slides de bônus exclusivos com scarcity (20 vagas)
[35:25] "Now you have the uh the bonuses. So um I don't know where the morality lines or the the morality lies as far as 20 spots open. Um I think everybody in the info space does this fake scarcity thing."
[35:38] "So you have 20 spots open for uh for all the special bonuses. I don't know. you know, sometimes it it glitches and we have a thousand people buy. That's not my fault. It's it's the software."
[35:50] "Um, so now again, you go into the bonuses and uh you're just making sure the value exceeds the price that you just pitched them at."
[36:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Everything You're Getting Tonight"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Recap completo: curso principal + todos os bônus
[36:02] "So after you go through the handful of bonuses, here's everything you're getting tonight. And this is after the so the offer goes live um right when they right when they get to that slide."
[36:15] "So, um, I'm always like moderating the back end of our of our clients webinars. Honestly, I should probably have a VA do this, but either way, it's kind of fun. It's like game day, like football night or Thursday night football every week."
[36:30] "Um, but so I'm in the back. I'm like making sure that anybody that's talking shit is getting kicked out. I'm making sure that the offer goes live. I'm like spamming the chat like, 'Yo, we got 20 spots open.'"
[36:42] "Uh, and then people start going to the to the sales page and buying right here uh after the the hourlong presentation and we and after the pitch."
[36:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Guarantee + Crossroads Close"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Garantia de satisfação + fechamento emocional de encruzilhada (você está aqui, escolha seu caminho)
[36:55] "Um, and you wrap everything up with a guarantee. Obviously, that that helps just continue to make the value exceed the price. And I like this crossroads close a lot. I use this in emails and VSSLs."
[37:08] "It's like you're here. You can now you've learned all this all this stuff. Like at the end of the webinar, you should have done you should have sold them on those three things. They understand the business model. They believe in themselves. they believe in you and uh now they're at this crossroads where they can keep doing the same thing they were doing and they can make make their life be the exact same way or they can invest a measly amount of money into changing their future and changing their life."
[37:38] "So after we kind of go through that process of the offer the emotional sale right there then we uh we engineer the Q&A to sell as well."
[37:48] "So, I'd say as as of right now in this part of the pitch, you'll get like your first 30% of customers like they're they're just ready to go. They they had their credit card out 5 minutes ago and they've already purchased."
[38:02] "But the Q&A is as important as as anything. Um like you could in theory you could end the webinar right now like, 'Okay, I just pitched like let's let's wrap it up.'"
[38:12] "Um, but if you do the Q&A well, and again, this is where your client or your charisma really comes into play as well, um, then you can make another 70% or another 80% more money."
[38:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Q&A Strategy"
NÚMEROS: 30% convert before Q&A, 70-80% more during Q&A
VISUAL: Estratégia de Q&A: FAQs primeiro → Live Q&A → Stories in answers → Scarcity updates → Welcome purchasers
[38:28] "Um, so generally we'll start off with FAQs. So, we finish the the offer, the whole presentation's done. What are the the frequently asked questions that we hear most of the time on these webinars? Just so we can control the narrative."
[38:42] "Uh because a lot of people are going to have the same questions. Everybody always likes to ask about taxes, which is like you're not even making any fuck money. Like why are you worried about taxes?"
[38:52] "But um we'll have like little questions like that that we'll preframe or pre-answer. Um and then we'll open up the live Q&A and the way that we have our clients answer these questions are very important."
[39:05] "So, first they're always like they'll answer a few questions and then they'll say, 'Okay, we got 17 spots left. Who's who's joining tonight? Who's becoming a a member tonight?'"
[39:15] "Um, so they're just continuously steering people back to the offer to the sales page. And u instead of so they'll directly answer questions plus they'll add a little storytelling into the way that they answer these questions."
[39:30] "So it's like somebody has a basic question, they'll say, 'Okay, yeah, this is how you handle this.' And this is actually exactly what our student Ian thought the same thing or this is what I used to struggle with as well, but I got over that by doing this."
[39:45] "So again, it just makes it more personal. It makes you it helps the audience put themselves in the shoes of uh of the presenter."
[39:55] "So obviously again the scarcity thing um 20 spots I'm like on the back end ticking down the spots as as we start to sell. Um so like that it's a big big asset or aspect as well is is that scarcity that you can uh you can have on these live webinars."
[40:12] "And then last is you kind of create some FOMO. You welcome in the purchasers and um you like call them out by name. You're going to change your life blah blah blah."
[40:22] "like it it creates FOMO if they if you see other people that that have enough trust in what they just saw to spend money you might be more it's like on uh in live seminars like if if at the end of the seminar people will pay they'll have people that'll like if Russell Brunson hosts a seminar and then he has an offer at the end he's going to have people that are fuck running over to the stand to buy the offer and that will innately make more people run over to the stand to buy the offer just because other people are doing it."
[40:52] "So, it's the same concept here."
[40:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Recap: Live Presentation is Only Half the Battle"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Resumo de pontos-chave: Traffic > Everything, Don't Reinvent the Wheel
[40:58] "Um, so just to recap, the live presentation is only half the battle. Um, like I I showed you in towards the beginning is like the majority of the work is in getting people to show up, getting them to sign up and getting them to show up. You need them to to show up and you need them to be excited in the right mental state. You need to frame it the right way."
[41:18] "Um, and then it's not going to work without traffic. Like, if there's one thing you take away from this, it's figure out how to get more traffic into your business."
[41:28] "Like that's that's the difference between the the clients that we work with that are making 600k a month and the guys that that we work with that or the guys that we turn away. Um it's simply eyeballs. If if you have enough eyeballs, everything will work."
[41:45] "Um anything will work really. Like you I hate to even say this, but like we're our attention to detail as an agency, as a service provider could could be better. Like as you've seen, we're not tracking numbers the way that we the way that other people do."
[42:00] "Um, but because we find these situations where like our mechanism is a perfect fit for our client and our client's a perfect fit for us, it can kind of outweigh the lack of of systems and the lack of of attention to detail in some cases."
[42:15] "Um obviously I think over time as you're in business you you attention to detail is a very important skill but again traffic solves everything."
[42:25] "Um and then lastly you don't need to reinvent the wheel. So like I said in the beginning like everything that I just showed you was regurgitated from from Russell Brunson and Jason Fladley."
[42:35] "And sure, there there's nuances that I've learned and there's stuff that I've learned that's that's helped me, but like the core fundamental principles of webinars and marketing in general, just just get them right. Like it's it's all the same thing."
[42:50] "Um especially on these live webinars. And that's like really what this has exposed to me is it's all like our slides. Anytime we sign a new client, um we have the same framework. we have the same system."
[43:02] "It's like the bare bones are already done. We just need to fill in the other 30% for the exact offer and the exact story that this client has."
[43:12] "Um, so like this has really helped me understand that when you just like double down on the basics, you double down on what's working, you have the same meal for lunch and dinner for the last 12 masterminds, um, then then you can't fuck it up."
[43:28] "That's that's the way to ensure that at the bare minimum you you do all right. Um and you're not trying to to reinvent the wheel."
[43:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "If You Got No Value - Watch This"
NÚMEROS: Não especificados
VISUAL: Link para YouTube video de Jason Fladley + recomendação do livro "One to Many"
[43:40] "Um last thing, if you got no value out of this, I wanted to to give you this. So uh I can send these slides or uh get you this link. This is just a YouTube video of Jason Fladley and talking about this is like the main thing that I watched uh when I started getting into the webinar space and then his book one to many."
[43:58] "Um, it'll be everything that I just talked about plus some more. And uh, just in case this bombed and you guys didn't like any of it here, I'm leaving with you, leaving you guys with this value. So, it's all we got."
[44:12] "Good job. Does anybody have any questions for Ben?"
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
$7+ milhões gerados para clientes em 2023
$200K-$400K/mês (maioria dos clientes)
$600K+/mês (maior cliente)
$170K-$180K (maior webinar individual - crypto offer)
Webinars individuais: $32K, $26K, $35K, $34K, $50K, $43K, $41K
90-100 calls agendadas em um único webinar (oferta small business)
6 figuras coletadas do primeiro webinar (cliente small business)
Agência cobra: $15K + 15% de comissão
ESTRUTURA DE KPIs:
Registrantes: 300-500 mínimo (idealmente 1000+)
Show-up rate: 30-50% (orgânico pode chegar a 50%)
Conversion rate: 5-10% (venda direta), 10%+ (agendamento de calls)
Cálculo exemplo: 1000 registrantes → 300 aparecem → 15 compram → $15K coletado ($1K offer)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Modelo de Oferta:
Mid-ticket: $500-$2,000 (venda direta no webinar)
High-ticket: $3K-$10K (agendamento de call)
Ascension ladder: mid-ticket primeiro, depois high-ticket
Trust Hour Rule:
10 minutos (short form content) vs 60-90 minutos (webinar)
Speed date concept: construção acelerada de confiança
Mais tempo = mais trust = mais vendas
Estrutura do Funil:
Traffic → Landing Page (ClickFunnels)
Opt-in (Webinar Jam embed)
Confirmation page (Telegram/IG Broadcast Channel)
Email + SMS nurture
Live webinar
Post-webinar sequences
Email Nurture:
Confirmation imediata
Story email
Social proof
YouTube videos
Reminders: 48h, 24h, 12h, 3h
Live link
SMS Nurture:
Confirmation
Day-of 12PM "game day"
1 hour reminder
15 minute reminder
Live now
10 minute "Hail Mary"
Estrutura do Webinar Live:
Horário: 7PM Eastern
Dias: Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Sunday
Frequência: Weekly ou bi-weekly
Flow do Webinar:
Welcome + Music (casual, vinho, conversa)
Pre-frame (valor, $2500 worth)
Chat engagement ("comment YES")
Quick story (credibilidade)
Social proof inicial
Training (5-step framework típico)
Summary
Longer story (jornada completa)
More social proof
Gift positioning
Value stack
Offer drop
Bonuses (20 spots - fake scarcity)
Guarantee + Crossroads close
FAQs
Live Q&A (70-80% das vendas acontecem aqui)
Welcome purchasers (FOMO)
3 Vendas Essenciais:
Mechanism/Business Model - Funciona? É sustentável?
You/Authority - Por que você? Relatabilidade + credibilidade
Them/Belief System - Social proof que eles podem fazer
Q&A Engineering:
30% convertem antes do Q&A
70-80% convertem durante Q&A
FAQs controlam narrativa
Respostas incluem storytelling
Updates constantes de scarcity
Welcome purchasers by name (FOMO)
Princípios de Relatabilidade:
"Ugly slides, pretty profits"
Quanto mais casual, melhor
Evitar pedestal profissional demais
Garrafa de vinho, música, conversa natural
Não parecer inacessível
Quem NÃO deve fazer webinars:
Sem traffic/audience suficiente
Personality boring (snoozefest)
Menos de 300-500 registrantes potenciais
Processamento de Pagamentos:
Início: Stripe
Escala: NMI (evitar chargebacks/fees)
Alternativa: Crypto
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Modelo de agência: $15K setup + 15% ongoing
ROI esperado primeiro webinar: 2-5x
Breakeven: 300-500 registrantes mínimo
Offer examples: $497, $700, $1000, $2500, $3K-$10K
ESTRUTURA:
Plataforma: Webinar Jam
Landing pages: ClickFunnels
Comunidade: Telegram ou IG Broadcast Channel
Backend moderation: Manual (deveria ser VA)
Framework: 70% template padrão + 30% customização
Baseado em: Russell Brunson + Jason Fladlin (One to Many, $100M+ em webinars)
FILOSOFIA:
Traffic solves everything
Don't reinvent the wheel
Double down on basics
Mesma refeição, mesmos resultados
Attention to detail < Product-market fit
Want the mechanism to fit the client perfectly
DURAÇÃO: ~44 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação ao vivo em mastermind/evento
